content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 ,J Street• Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Bryan Bondy 
1048 8 Graham Ct. 
Ventura, CA 93004 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-17-106 
Dear Mr. Bondy: 
May 26,2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
Government Code section 1090. Your request involves a consultant contract between you and the 
City of San Buenaventura for a hydro geological study related to a potable reuse project. In our 
telephone conversation of May 16, 2017, you stated that the City sent you a request for proposal, 
and that you submitted a proposal to the City. You have since received notification from the City 
that you are the preferred contractor for the project. 
Although the Commission has the jurisdiction to issue advice under Section 1090, we are 
unable to issue such advice where, as here, it relates to past conduct. (Section 1097.l(c)(2).) 
Accordingly, we decline to issue advice in this matter and consider the request withdrawn. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
ECR:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
,4-,-J 
Emelyn C. Rodriguez 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion: Although the Commission has the jurisdiction to issue advice under Section 1090, we are unable to issue such advice where, as here, it relates to past conduct. (Section 1097.l(c)(2).) Accordingly, we decline to issue advice in this matter and consider the request withdrawn.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a consultant contract between the requestor and the City of San Buenaventura for a hydrogeological study related to a potable reuse project creates a conflict of interest under Government Code section 1090.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to issue advice because the request relates to past conduct, which falls outside the Commission's authority to provide prospective advice under Section 1090.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter where the FPPC declines to issue advice due to temporal limitations on its jurisdiction (past conduct rather than prospective). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present, but the letter contains clear statements of the legal issue and the reason for declining advice.

CONCLUSION: Although the Commission has the jurisdiction to issue advice under Section 1090, we are unable to issue such advice where, as here, it relates to past conduct. (Section 1097.l(c)(2).) Accordingly, we decline to issue advice in this matter and consider the request withdrawn.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to issue advice regarding a potential conflict of interest under Government Code section 1090 concerning a consultant contract with the City of San Buenaventura, finding that the request relates to past conduct and therefore withdrew the request.
